However it presents a significant challenge to current systems because the fine grain information can potentially expose sensitive details or violate privacy constraints imposed by users/organizations in their policies (e .g., non disclosure agreement). A naive solution would be encryption but this will require costly decryption during analysis which may make them unfeasible especially if performed frequently over large amounts of events with long latencies like real time applications e g , distributed tracing frameworks etc..

Support vector machine (SVM) has achieved many successes in machine learning, especially for a small sample data. However, it is still a challenge to obtain the global optimal solution of SVM with quadratic programming (QP), which is very time-consuming and memory-consuming. To address this issue, we propose a new fast approximate method, namely FAST-SVM, based on the sparse kernel trick. The proposed method can effectively improve the efficiency of solving optimization problems by using the sparse representation to accelerate the calculation of the gradient function of the objective function. In addition, we also use the low-rank approximation method to reduce the memory overhead of the algorithm. Finally, the numerical experiments show that our proposed method can achieve similar results

We present the first batch Bayesian Optimization algorithm that achieves a provably efficient regret-to-sigma ratio (TS-RSR) using Thompson Sampling (TS). Our algorithm is based on a novel parametrization of the Gaussian process (GP) prior, which we call the normalized basis parametrization (NBP). We show that the TS-RSR is minimized for a particular choice of the GP hyperparameters, and we use this insight to build a NBP-GP model from data. We then prove that the regret of our batch Bayesian Optimization algorithm is upper bounded by $O(\frac{\log T}{\sqrt{nT}})$, where $T$ is the optimization horizon and $n$ is the number of batch points. We also show that our algorithm is minimax optimal, in the sense that our lower bound on the regret is order optimal in $T$ for a fixed $n$. We finally show that our algorithm can be efficiently implemented, in a way that is amenable to parallelization. We compare our algorithm to the state of the art in batch Bayesian Optimization on several benchmark problems.

We present a new extension for Neural Optimal Transport (NOT) training procedure, capable of accurating the results in terms not only on transport cost but also entropy regularization. We achieve this by introducing an additional loss term to NOT's objective function and optimizing it jointly with other parameters using gradient descent methods such as Adam or SGD . Through experiments we demonstrate that our approach yields significantly better performance than existing approaches when trained over multiple epochs without sacrificing speedup gains compared previous works which require more expensive optimization algorithms like LBFGS/CGM etc.. Furthermore ,we show how these improvements can be leveraged towards solving challenging problems involving large datasets where traditional techni

We extend the seminal model of Pathak and S\"onmez (2008) to a large class of models, characterizing conditions under which an equilibrium in such games is unique. Large classes include models with a finite number of equilibria, and models where there are more equilibria than strategies for any agent; moreover, our results hold if the game has only one player or two players. In particular, we show that the existence of a unique equilibrium is guaranteed when the game satisfies a simple-to-verify condition called \emph{local monotonicity}, and this result holds even without assuming that the utility functions are convex. Our work also reveals that the concept of local monotonicity is robust enough to capture a variety of properties, such as the existence of a dominant strategy, Nash stabili

Cyber threats continue to evolve in complexity, thereby traditional Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) methods struggle to keep pace. AI offers a potential solution, automating and enhancing various tasks, from data ingestion to resilience verification. This paper explores the potential of integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into CTI. We provide a blueprint of an AI-enhanced CTI processing pipeline, and detail its components and functionalities. The pipeline highlights the collaboration of AI and human expertise, which is necessary to produce timely and high-fidelity cyber threat intelligence. We also explore the automated generation of mitigation recommendations, harnessing AI's capabilities to provide real-time, contextual, and predictive insights. However, the integration of AI into C

How political beliefs change in accordance with media exposure is a complicated matter. Some studies have found that individuals become more politically engaged after consuming news, while others suggest the opposite effect: disengagement or alienation from politics altogether (e., Gnip et al.). However limited to specific aspects of online and offline engagement—for example, participation in protests versus voting behavior—, these results often fail at capturing how an individual’s overall involvement shifts over time as they interact across different channels like TV broadcasts vs podcasts streaming platforms etc.. The present research investigates this issue by examining survey responses among citizens who were exposed either primarily through traditional broadcast television programmin

Chinese web search engines return a list of search results without providing summary information. In contrast, foreign web search engines return search results with summary information extracted from the full text of web pages. The difference may be attributed to the development of natural language processing (NLP) technologies suited for the extraction of summary information from full text. In this paper, we analyze the possibility that the underdevelopment of NLP technologies for Chinese is due to the lack of annotated data, and create a new dataset with annotated citations and full texts of web pages for training an extraction model. We then propose a novel algorithm, called WebCiteS, for extracting query-focused summaries from the full texts of web pages on the basis of the citations. The new algorithm extends our previous work, WebCite (Liu et al. 2020), which extracted bibliographical information of citations. The extracted summaries are evaluated subjectively by 5 researchers and objectively by 3 search engines. The results show that the average subjective score increases by 1.29 points (on a 5-point scale) and the objective recall increases by 1.4 times compared with the previous method.

Despite the vast repository of global medical knowledge predominantly being in English, local languages are crucial for effective communication and understanding. This is particularly significant in countries like India, where the majority of the population speaks regional languages, posing challenges in accessibility to healthcare information. However, despite this disparity, the advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) and natural language processing (NLP) technology has paved the way for machine translation (MT). This paper presents a comprehensive survey on AI-based machine translation solutions in the medical domain. We delineate the key challenges related to MT and focus on NLP techniques, including morphological analysis, part-of-speech tagging, and named entity recognition, that

The fusion models, which effectively combine information from different sources, are widely used in solving multimodal problems. However, they lack robustness to adversarial attacks and do not perform well when the model is attacked by unknown input data. In this paper, we propose a novel method of attacking multimodal fusion models based on Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) combined with the Attack-and-Defend strategy to generate diverse adversarial samples. Specifically, an attacker first generates samples to attack the fusion model by using GAN, then the defender uses these generated samples to train the fusion model for defense. To verify our method, we implement experiments under multiple scenarios, including multimodal image classification and visual question answering. We find th

In recent years, few-shot and zero-shot learning, which learn to predict labels with limited or no training data respectively (Vinyals et al., 2016; Ravi & Larochelle, ), have become popular approaches for dealing with the ever increasing number of classes in image recognition tasks. These methods can be applied on top a large pretrained model such as ResNet54 trained from ImageNet datasets containing thousands images per class but only tens thousand labeled examples are required at inference time due their ability exploit information learned during pretraining without requiring additional fine tuning steps when faced new unseen categories . However , these techniques suffer significant performance degradation if they're used directly because most objects don’t share enough visual featur

Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are currently among the most popular machine learning models, and stochastic gradient descent (SGD) is one of the most widely used algorithms for DNNs optimization. However, recent research has shown that SGD converges very slowly for modern DNNs. The Hessian matrix of a DNN describes the curvature of the loss function and can provide information about the local geometry of the optimization landscape. In this paper, we propose to approximate the Hessian matrix by selecting a subset of all the parameters of a DNN and to use the SGD algorithm to minimize the loss function. We refer to this method as SGD with partial Hessian. We theoretically show that, under mild conditions, SGD with partial Hessian converges to a global minimum at a linear rate. Moreover, we empirically show that SGD with partial Hessian accelerates the convergence of SGD significantly for modern DNNs.

Adversarial attacks on graph neural networks (GNNs) are a rapidly developing subfield of adversarial machine learning, motivated by the observation that GNNs are vulnerable to adversarial attacks and have become a popular tool for improving the robustness of GNNs. Most existing GNN adversarial attacks are based on gradient-descent-based optimization, which aims to perturb the adjacency matrix or node features of the graph structure. However, such a perturbation may cause a large change in the topology of the graph, resulting in a poor attack effect. Therefore, we propose a minimum topology attack (MTA) based on a relaxation of the combinatorial optimization problem, which aims to find a small set of edges whose removal can make the GNN model predict incorrectly. Unlike existing attacks that rely on gradient-descent-based optimization, we propose an efficient subgradient-based optimization algorithm to solve the relaxed problem, which can find a good approximate solution quickly. The solution can be further improved by a local search algorithm. Compared with existing state-of-the-art attacks, MTA can achieve a higher attack success rate (ASR) on most benchmark datasets.

This article proposes a novel Children-Computer Interaction (CCI) approach for the task of handwriting recognition using a digital stylus on a touchscreen. Our approach is designed to overcome common challenges in CCI, including the lack of standardization and data diversity among existing datasets. To address these issues, we introduce our new dataset called Handwriting4Children, which contains 190k samples collected from 380 children aged between 5 and 12 years old. The dataset covers 20 commonly used characters and is annotated with segmentation masks and keypoints. We then present two CCI models: a CNN-based model utilizing keypoints as input and a Transformer-based model incorporating segmentation masks as input. Both models are trained and evaluated on the Handwriting4Children datase

Channel attention mechanisms endeavor to recalibrate channel weights to enhance representation abilities of channel filters. The existing works in this domain mainly focus on the feature-level channel attention mechanism, which is designed to adjust weights for each feature channel separately. In contrast, the recent work by Chen et al. [2023] introduces a global channel attention mechanism that utilizes an encoder-decoder framework and a novel parameter-sharing scheme to address cross-channel dependencies. However, the effectiveness of their method is still limited when the channel attention scope is small, e.g., only one or two layers are involved. To mitigate this drawback, we introduce a novel context-aware channel attention (CACA) module based on a transformer structure.

The open radio access network (O-RAN) offers new degrees of freedom for building and operating mobile networks, but this also presents significant challenges. For example, the current focus on 4G/5Gs has been to optimize performance at layer one in terms such as throughput or latency; however with O‐RANS there is an opportunity now that we can start thinking about optimizing our operations across layers two through four which would lead us towards a more efficient use case model where each service could be tailored accordingly based upon its requirements rather than having them all run off similar templates like today's models do so well! To address these issues by leveraging AI technologies into your business processes—from customer experience management systems up until inventory control

Visual entailment (VE) is a multimodal reasoning task consisting of image-sententence pairs, where the target sentence may be inferred from one or more given supporting sentences. VE has attracted considerable attention as an important benchmark for visual and textual learning systems that aims to explore how machine reason visually with language understanding capabilities in daily scenarios such like video captioning / summarization tasks by leveraging natural languages information together along side videos features [1]. To better understand this problem we have studied several previous works on Visual Entailments which will help us further improve our model performance. We found out two main issues: i The existing models mainly focus only vision aspect instead paying mu

As the hardware acceleration of machine learning (ML) algorithms continues to evolve, there is a growing need to develop communication architectures that can scale-out to support increasing computational demands without incurring prohibitively high energy costs. In this paper, we investigate the potential of silicon photonic interposers to overcome communication bottlenecks in future ML hardware accelerators. We present a 2.5D photonic interposer network architecture that is capable of connecting multiple ML accelerators to an optical switch fabric. We show that our photonic interposers can realize low-loss, high-bandwidth connections between the ML accelerators and the switch fabric, with energy efficiencies that are two to three orders of magnitude higher than electrical interconnects. We demonstrate the functionality of our architecture by experimentally evaluating a silicon photonic interposer that connects a ML accelerator board to an optical switch. The results of the experiments carried out on this proof-of-concept interposer demonstrate its ability to forward data packets with low loss and high bandwidth, and confirm the feasibility of our 2.5D photonic interposers for use in scalable ML hardware accelerators.

3D visual grounding involves matching natural language descriptions with their corresponding objects in 3D scenes. It is challenging due to the complex semantic and geometric relations between words, phrases or sentences as well instances of classes from different viewpoints within a scene graph representation (i). In this paper we propose an effective solution based on transformer networks for efficient self-supervised learning that addresses these challenges by using two simple yet powerful strategies: object tagging via spatial attention masking; class bias reduction through multitask contrastive training objectives across both image pairs drawn at random angles around ground truth instance locations identified during inference time step(ii) Our model outperforms other stateofart method

Location awareness is a critical issue in wireless sensor network applications. For more accurate location estimation, we propose a novel method called localization based on the weighted average of the distance (LWAD). This technique uses a weighted average of distance measurements obtained from multiple access points to determine the estimated device position. In contrast to traditional methods that rely solely on Euclidean distance, LWAD uses a distance-based weighting function to incorporate the impact of distance on accuracy and precision. To effectively estimate distances between devices and access points, we employ the concept of virtual directional antennas. These antennas are designed to mimic the propagation patterns of the actual antennas employed by the access points. The use of

Visible-infrared person re-identification (VI-ReID) is challenging due to the diverse appearance of people caused by the complex background and lighting conditions. Recent methods have achieved remarkable progress in VI-ReID, mainly based on deep learning approaches. However, most existing methods focus on the feature extraction and representation learning process for person images. In this paper, we propose a novel framework named Cross-Modality Contrastive Learning with Transformer for VI-ReID (CMCLT), which learns cross-modality representations through contrastive learning within and across modalities. Specifically, we introduce the contrastive loss function with hard positive and negative pairs in the feature space for each modality. Moreover, we design a transformer-based model to gen

Out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization in graphs is an important yet challenging task in graph learning. It is especially challenging when the test graphs have not been observed in the training phase. In this work, we propose a novel OOD generalization method via hierarchical semantic environments (HSE). The key idea is to progressively refine the semantic information of the graph structure by leveraging the hierarchical structure commonly existing in real-world graphs. Specifically, we first propose to embed hierarchical semantic concepts into graphs via a semantic edge generator. This generator learns a semantic space and populates semantic edges between nodes with similar semantic meanings. We then propose a semantic message passing module to aggregate semantic information in a hierarchical manner. Finally, we develop a semantic perturbation module to further enhance the model's robustness against OOD graphs. Extensive experiments on various benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed HSE method achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of OOD generalization. Furthermore, we also show that HSE can be seamlessly integrated with other graph learning models to improve their OOD generalization performance.

Complex networks are widely used to represent an abundance of real-world relations ranging from social networks to protein interactions. The emergence and success of complex network theory can be attributed to the simplicity and universality of its mathematical representation, which consists of nodes and edges connecting pairs of nodes. However, the binary nature of edges limits our understanding of complex network dynamics, particularly in scenarios where nodes exchange multiple types of information with each other. To address this issue, we propose a novel framework that captures and models multivariate interactions between nodes based on their positions in space and time. In particular, we model the multivariate relations as a matrix whose elements depend on the relative positions of th

Detecting edges in images suffers from the problems of (P1) heavy imbalance, i.e., only a small fraction (< 5%) of pixels belong to edge class and many more are background; P2: noisy data with significant noise on some inputs due their low-resolution or poor quality image sensors leading into blurred regions around true boundaries that could be misclassified as well if not handled carefully enough during processing steps like filtering etc. ; Finally there is also an issue when dealing specifically toward detecting certain types such s curves which can easily go unnoticed without proper attention paid towards them beforehand To address these issues we propose two novel approaches for addressing detection task using Deep Convolutional Neural Network(DCNNs).

Cloud native technologies have been observed to expand into the realm of Internet of Things (IoT) in recent years. The adoption and implementation of cloud native technologies in IoT is referred to as Cloud Native IoT (CNIoT). CNIoT provides a more efficient, reliable, scalable, and secure way for IoT devices to connect with the internet. This article aims to provide an overview of how cloud native technologies are being implemented in the IoT landscape by reviewing various articles from reputable journals and conferences, as well as a survey conducted on existing research papers related to this topic. We identify three key areas where cloud native technologies are being utilized in IoT: infrastructure, edge computing, and security. For each area, we discuss the challenges and opportunitie

The recent explosion in popularity of large language models (LLMs) has inspired learning engine architectures that leverage these powerful tools as an effective knowledge source. However, existing approaches face several key challenges: they require a significant amount and variety data to train the model; it is difficult for users with limited technical expertise or domain-specific training datasets access this kind information from LLMS efficiently using traditional search engines due their focus on text retrieval rather than understanding contextually relevant questions posed by humans through natural conversations such those found within human interactions between friends via social media platforms like Twitter & Facebook Messenger chats etc., which can lead not only misinterpretations

Denoising diffusion probabilistic models (DDPMs) are becoming the leading paradigm for image generation. However, while DDPMs have been shown to be effective at generating high-quality images, they lack the ability to generate images with a particular style. To address this limitation, we introduce StyleGPT, a novel approach that leverages the power of large language models (LLMs) to enhance the generation capabilities of DDPMs by incorporating textual descriptions in the generation process. Specifically, our model uses LLM prompts and their embeddings as additional conditioning information during the sampling process, enabling the generation of images that conform to textual descriptions provided by users. We evaluate the effectiveness of StyleGPT through several experiments on different

Many computer vision and machine learning problems are modelled as learning tasks on graphs, where graph neural networks (GNNs) have emerged to be powerful tool for solving them. Graph convolutions form the core of GSN models with two main types: spectral-based methods that perform convolution in feature space using eigenvectors obtained by applying Laplace operators or its variants; spatial based techniques which operate directly over features without requiring any additional transformations such those used within Spectral Based Methods(SBM). In this work we provide an overview about both approaches alongwith their advantages & disadvantages while comparing results across datasets commonly employed during evaluation process including MNIST digit recognition dataset consisting 10 classes e

Successful job search results from job seekers' well-shaped social communication. While well-known skills such as teamwork, problem solving and conflict management are critical for success in the workplace but often neglected during university education (O'Reilly et al., 2017). The present study aims to address this gap by investigating whether students who have had a meaningful experience of working with people outside their academic setting would have better employment outcomes than those without these experiences ("nonacademic work" group vs "no nonwork"). Using data collected through an online survey at two time points: one year before graduation; three months after commencement ceremony) among Chinese undergraduate business majors $(\mathrm{N}=564)$, we found that both groups were equ

Visual imitation learning has achieved impressive progress in learning unimanual manipulation tasks from a small set of demonstrations. However, the majority of existing methods focus on solving a single task and do not generalize to new tasks or novel environments. To address this issue, we propose Cogent, an effective framework that can learn multi-task policies using only one training dataset containing multiple tasks. This is achieved through the introduction of a shared latent space, which enables information sharing among different tasks during training. Additionally, we employ an explicit alignment mechanism between tasks, allowing for efficient transfer learning while preserving important task differences. Extensive experiments conducted across five robotic manipulation tasks demon

While there have been numerous advances in natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning (ML), many tasks in biomedical text mining (BMTM) continue to present significant challenges. Identifying and prioritizing these open challenges is an important first step towards advancing BMTM research. Towards this goal, we organized a workshop titled "Challenges in Biomedical Text Mining" (CBMTM 2021) as a satellite event of the 14th International Symposium on Bioinformatics (ISB 2021). The workshop brought together leading researchers in the field to discuss and identify key challenges in BMTM, and to propose feasible solutions to address them. In this paper, we present the outcomes of the workshop, including a curated list of 43 challenges in six categories, namely (1) data, (2) evaluation, (3) explainability, (4) fairness, (5) privacy, and (6) multi-modality. We also introduce Community Challenges, a new format that aims to engage the entire BMTM community -- from early career researchers to established experts -- in tackling these challenges.

Recent growth in the popularity of large language models has led to their increased usage for summarizing text. However, most existing systems are not interpretable or provide only partial explanations as they ignore both lexical and semantic information that can be extracted from a summary's content words by leveraging pre-trained word embeddings like BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) . To address this issue we propose an interactive summarization system built upon our proposed Interpretability Enhancement Framework(IEF).

Collaborative competitions have gained popularity in the scientific and technological fields. These competitions foster innovation by encouraging participants to work together, rather than competing against one another. In this paper, we introduce a novel collaborative competition framework called CoCoCom. This framework aims to enhance the efficiency of existing competitive events by providing an additional layer of collaboration. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through the development of a multi-robot system for a robotic soccer competition. By introducing collaboration mechanisms within this context, we showcase how teams can effectively coordinate their actions, leading to improved performance and outcomes. Our findings highlight the potential benefits of incorporating

Machine Learning (ML) has replaced traditional handcrafted methods for perception and prediction in autonomous systems. However, ML models are notoriously opaque with regards to their internal decision process which makes it difficult to interpret the cause of a particular output or detect when an error occurs due e..g., input data drift from training time distribution assumptions . This is especially problematic since errors can have devastating consequences if they occur while running on safety-critical applications such as self driving cars where people's lives depend upon them making correct decisions at all times under uncertain conditions including changing environments over extended periods without supervision by humans during operation phase after deployment into real world environ

With the rapid development of deep learning techniques, image processing technologies have also made great progress. How to evaluate the quality of the generated images is still an open question. This paper uses a generative adversarial network to generate modern Chinese landscape photos. Due to the limitation of data, it is difficult to generate high-quality images that can fool human beings. We use a two-stage training strategy to improve the quality of the generated images. We find that the discriminator can be used as a feature extractor and the perceptual loss can be used to improve the quality of the generated images. The generated images are evaluated by professionals and non-professionals, and the results show that the generated images can fool non-professionals but not professionals.

Despite the vast repository of global medical knowledge predominantly being in English, local languages are crucial for delivering tailored healthcare services, particularly in areas with limited medical resources. To extend the reach of medical AI advancements to a broader population, we aim to develop medical LLMs across the six most widely spoken languages, encompassing a global population of 6.1 billion. This effort culminates in the creation of the ApolloCorpora multilingual medical dataset and the XMedBench benchmark. In the multilingual medical benchmark, the released Apollo models, at various relatively-small sizes (i.e., 0.5B, 1.8B, 2B, 6B, and 7B), achieve the best performance among models of equivalent size.

In this paper we describe the development of a streamlined framework for large-scale ATLAS p+p data analysis. The workflow is composed of three components: (1) an asynchronous multithreading mechanism to speed up parallel operations in Python, which allows us access to all CPUs on our servers; and(2),(3),an automated script scheduler that submits jobs basedon availabilityof resourcesand their prioritylevel at SLAC’s Condor cluster facility as wellas ATLAS computing centers aroundthe world via GridManuver software developed by Jefferson Lab users.

Masked image modeling (MIM), which predicts randomly masked patches from unmasked images, has recently emerged as a powerful methodology for learning generalizable representations. In this paper, we propose a novel approach to MIM based on the self-distillation technique. We demonstrate that MIM can be effectively applied to a variety of downstream vision tasks by using an auxiliary model as a teacher. Specifically, our proposed method consists of two stages: a mask sampling stage and a distillation stage. The mask sampling stage randomly samples masks from all pixels in an input image and computes a corresponding loss. The distillation stage uses the auxiliary model as a teacher to supervise the prediction of the masked patches. Our model has two advantages compared with existing approach

We study reinforcement learning with linear function approximation, unknown transition, and adversarial losses in the general setting of finite horizon zero sum games. In our results we obtain an explicit characterization for the value of a policy in terms of the expected return of that policy. Our main result is that a policy is optimal if and only if it maximizes the average reward achieved by playing against any randomized opponent, up to a constant multiplicative factor, under a specific condition on the behavior policy of the opponent. This is a natural extension of the result of \cite{bhandari2021reinforcement} where this property was shown for policies satisfying linear constraints. As a corollary of our results we show that, when the policy is known, there exists a policy which is

A common issue for machine learning models applied to time-series forecasting is the temporal evolution of feature importance. To address this, we present a method that incorporates adaptive tuning by combining multiple models with varying levels and complexities using an ensemble framework as well one or more base learners such us linear regression (LR) model tuned on historical data points over different periods in order predict future values accurately without sacrificing accuracy too much due its simplicity relative other methods available today This approach allows users select which combination best suits their needs: whether they want something simple but effective enough -or complex yet precise when dealing large datasets containing many features whose significance changes accordi

Autonomous navigation is one of the key requirements for every potential application of mobile robots in the industry. As a result, autonomous robot localization has become an active research area over the past few decades and remains to be so even today as it helps overcome limitations associated with sensor placement or other environmental factors affecting positioning accuracy [1]. Robot Localization refers generally to determining its position relative either (a) based on global map information using computer vision techniques such that pose estimation becomes possible without any prior knowledge about local environment features like landmarks; this approach can also include motion planning which involves finding optimal path between two points given certain constraints e.,g distance t

The capabilities of large language models (LLMs) have been progressing at a breathtaking pace. However, the field still faces challenges in ensuring that these models operate responsibly and safely in real-world applications. To address this issue, we propose a novel framework for LLMs, called "Ethical Responsible Model" (ERM). ERM is an approach that integrates ethics into the model design process to ensure that LLMs are aligned with societal values and norms. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of ERM, including its motivation, key components, potential benefits, and limitations. We also discuss current research on ERM and provide future directions for this emerging field. By leveraging state-of-the-art technologies like AI generative models, we aim to create a more responsible

Neural networks have become a cornerstone of machine learning. As the trend for these to get bigger and more complex continues, we need better algorithms that help us understand them in detail so as not be left behind by this rapid progress! This blog post aims at explaining how one can use linear algebra tools like matrices or vectors along with their properties (e g., rank) when working on neural nets—specifically those based around deep convolutional models such GoogLeNet/InceptionV3 which are used widely today due largely part because they offer very good performance across many different tasks including classification problems involving images among others types similar nature while still retaining reasonable computational complexity compared other approaches available nowadays especi

Recently, diffusion-based methods for monocular 3D human pose estimation have achieved state-of-the-art performance. However, existing approaches either lack the ability to generate a complete 3D human model with fine-grained details or suffer from blurry texture and poor semantic information. To address this issue, we propose a novel approach called Pose Diffusion 3D (PoseDiff3D), which leverages a hybrid diffusion network consisting of both a feature-space and an image-space diffusion process. In the feature space, we employ a fully connected encoder to extract meaningful features from both 2D image input and textual prompt, while in the image space, a 3D human mesh is conditioned on the extracted features via a mesh decoder, resulting in high-resolution 3D mesh output.

Feature selection aims to identify the most pattern-discriminative feature subset. In prior works, existing methods mainly focus on optimizing a single criterion for evaluating each selected candidate or using multiple criteria and then averaging their performance scores as an overall assessment score of candidates' discriminativeness without considering any possible correlations among them. Consequently these approaches are unable effectively evaluate individual features in terms that they can be considered simultaneously with other variables within data sets when selecting optimal subsets from large numbers containing many redundant/irrelevant attributes (features). This paper proposes novel algorithm called Simultaneous Multi Criteria Evaluation Feature Selection Algorithm(SMCEFS) which

Instruction Tuning has the potential to stimulate or enhance specific capabilities of large language models (LLMs) with a small amount and type-specific training data. However, existing methods rely on manual annotation for each task/type combination while requiring an LLM per instruction tuning method; thus making it difficult in practice as well costly from both time & resource perspectives especially when dealing across multiple types within one model setting like ours here - 10+ tasks spanning several domains such that we needed at least three separate versions: text generation + question answering plus summarization!

Molecular Communication (MC) has emerged as a promising paradigm employing molecular information carriers for communication and sensing in Nano-bio systems. Recently, it is observed that the channel model plays an important role on designing efficient protocols under different constraints such energy consumption or delay time of data transfer within nano/micro sized range where traditional wireless networks are not applicable due to their size limitations [1]. In this paper we focus mainly on design challenges related with application requirements arising from usage scenarios identified through recent literature survey focusing both biological applications like human body monitoring by implantable devices which can be used inside blood vessels using nanorobots carrying out medical interven

We derive new error bounds for the particle gradient descent algorithm, in both convex and non-convex settings. Our bounds improve on existing bounds when the algorithm is run for a number of iterations that is polynomial in the number of particles. We also extend two important inequalities from the theory of Markov chains, namely the log-Sobolev inequality and the Talagrand inequality, to the setting where the state space is discrete but has a continuum of particles.

Context: In the first part of his 2012 presentation "Inventing on Princi-ple," Apple CEO Tim Cook discussed the importance of keeping your eyes open for problems that need solving. He then went on to discuss how to solve these problems, and in particular, how to invent new things based on the principles of simplicity and thoughtfulness. ## Tim Cook's thoughts on problem-solving In his keynote speech at the 2012 All Things D conference, Tim Cook laid out a simple process for coming up with new ideas. It goes something like this: 1. First, you have to see the world as it is. This means understanding what people are doing now, and how they're using technology. You can do this by talking to customers, reading reviews, or just observing people in their natural environment. 2. Next, you have t

With the requirements of Intelligent Transport Systems (ITSs) for extensive connectivity of Electronics Control Units (ECUs), the increasing number of ECUs in a vehicle, and the need to meet stringent time-to-market and cost targets, there is an urgent requirement to develop an integrated approach to architecture design. This paper presents a new methodology for integrating model-based architectural engineering with model-driven development of software-intensive systems. The methodology consists of two main steps: (i) using a Model-Driven Architecture (MDA) to analyze and model the system under consideration based on its functional and non-functional requirements, and (ii) applying model transformations to derive a preliminary architecture that satisfies the system's requirements. To verif

A core ambition of reinforcement learning (RL) is the creation of agents capable of rapid adaptation to diverse environments. However, current RL methods rely on extensive interaction with an environment or large datasets for training. These methods are therefore not easily scalable and suffer from sample inefficiency, especially when dealing with novel tasks. In this paper, we propose a novel methodology called Adaptive Behaviour Learning (ABL), which leverages imitation learning for agent adaptation. Our approach learns behavioural models from demonstrations and employs a reward function derived from these models to guide behaviour. This allows the agent to adapt its behaviour by adjusting the parameters of its behavioural model, rather than requiring additional interactions with the env

We investigate the secure downlink transmission in a wireless network assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). The considered RIS-aided communication system consists of one access point (AP) and multiple users. The AP wishes to serve the users with a certain signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirement while minimizing the energy consumption of the RIS. Meanwhile, the users attempt to maximize their received signal power while satisfying the SINR requirement imposed by the AP. To solve the non-convex and multi-objective optimization problem, we propose a novel opportunistic user scheduling (OUS) algorithm by introducing a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) based approach. In particular, the AP and the users are modeled as agents in a MARL environment, where the AP aims to minimize the RIS energy consumption while the users try to maximize their received signal power. The OUS algorithm is then derived based on the principle of maximizing the minimum reward among all agents, which ensures a certain level of cooperation between the AP and the users. Simulation results show that the proposed OUS algorithm can satisfy the SINR requirements of all users while achieving significant power saving for the RIS compared to other benchmark schemes. In addition, the proposed OUS algorithm can provide a trade-off between power saving and the satisfaction level of the users.

Vision language models (VLMs) have experienced rapid advancements through the integration of large language model (LLM) pre-training. We observe that the LLM-based fine-tuning strategies for vision tasks are not standardized, which leads to inconsistent performance across datasets and downstream applications. In this paper, we propose a novel multi-step approach called STEP, which systematically optimizes the initial parameters and training schedules for various vision tasks. Specifically, we first derive an initial set of hyperparameters from extensive experiments on the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge (ILSVRC), where we find that early stopping is crucial and that model complexity has a positive correlation with performance. Based on these observations, we design a mult

We present a new class of implicit-explicit (IMEX) integrators based on the exponential time differencing (ETD) method for the numerical simulation of mass-spring-charge (MSC) systems. Our proposed IMEX-ETD integrators are unconditionally stable and exhibit excellent long-time numerical performance, while preserving the discrete energy and momentum of the underlying system. We demonstrate that IMEX-ETD outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on both benchmark and real-world MSC systems.

In this article, we study the relationship between notions of depth for sequences, namely, Bennett’s and Rogozin-Sazonov.
